title: Dasistoma serrata
taxonomy: Dasistoma serrata belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as broomrapes or Indian paintbrush. The Orobanchaceae family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Lamiales is a part of the clade Asterids, which includes a wide variety of flowering plants. Dasistoma serrata's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Dasistoma serrata, also known as Rough-leaved Dasistoma, is a perennial plant native to North America, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infection. The bioactive constituents of Dasistoma serrata include rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dasistoma serrata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly prepared by herbalists to promote digestive health and immune system function.
